title: 3 Stocks That Could Soar Under Inflationary Conditions
text: Investing in companies that benefit from rising prices can help you hedge your portfolio against weakness in the broader market.

title: 4 Oilfield Services Stocks to Gain From the Flourishing Industry
text: With drillers returning to the oil patches, demand for oilfield services has improved significantly, making the outlook for the Zacks Oil and Gas- Field Services industry bright again. Among the frontrunners that might make the most of the improving business scenario are SLB, HAL, BKR and PUMP.

title: Energy Stocks Fall as Much as 7%. They Are Leading the S&P 500 Lower.
text: Some big names, such as Halliburton and Exxon Mobil, are slumping on Monday.

title: Halliburton's Second Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Halliburton Company (NYSE: HAL) will host a conference call on Tuesday, July 19, 2022, to discuss its second quarter 2022 financial results. The call will begin at 8:00 AM Central Time (9:00 AM Eastern Time). The Company will issue a press release regarding the second quarter 2022 earnings prior to the conference call. title: HAL Stock Price Prediction: Why One Analyst Sees Halliburton Hitting $53
text: Today, one HAL stock price prediction has Wall Street and Main Street alike taking a look at energy company Halliburton. The post HAL Stock Price Prediction: Why One Analyst Sees Halliburton Hitting $53 appeared first on InvestorPlace.

title: Halliburton: This Time Is Not Different
text: Higher oil and gas prices should create a more favorable environment for oilfield service companies, at least in the short term. Halliburton is focused on margins rather than growth, for now. Growth, in addition to improved profitability is required to justify the current share price though.

title: 5 Growth Stocks That a Volatile Market Can't Stop
text: These growth stocks have outperformed the broader market by a wide margin this year, delivering outsized gains to shareholders. The post 5 Growth Stocks That a Volatile Market Can't Stop appeared first on InvestorPlace.

title: 3 Outperforming Energy Stocks with More in the Tank
text: The global energy sector climbed to new highs last week as crude oil and natural gas prices rose. Based on forward-looking earnings estimates and sell-side price targets, there appears to be a lot more in the tank for these three energy companies.
